implemented.notes: Next.js 14.2.3 static export, App Router, TypeScript strict, CSS custom properties (light/dark), 480px centered layout, EPOCH_DATE puzzle logic, full TypeScript type system
930 cards: 365 simple-daily, 365 cryptic-daily, 100 simple-practice, 100 cryptic-practice. Zero overlap between pools. EDHREC rank curation.
36,606 unique Oracle card names. 745KB uncompressed, 241KB gzipped (under 500KB AC met). Already implemented during foundation phase — verified REUSE, no new code needed.

parseMana('{2}{U}{B}') → ['2','U','B']. tokenCategory() classifies generic/colour/hybrid/phyrexian. colorIdentityEmoji() for share grid. <ManaSymbol token='U'/> and <ManaCost cost='{2}{U}{B}'/> SVG components. No external font dependency.

Home screen shows Simple #N and Cryptic #N daily puzzle options with descriptions, Practice mode entry, and T&Cs footer link. TierSelection is a client component that hydrates puzzle number from EPOCH_DATE on mount. Stub play routes created for /play/simple, /play/cryptic, /play/practice — game board will be implemented in clue-progression-engine story. Build produces 8 static routes, 95.9KB first-load JS on home.

6-round clue progression engine with CSS card frame (63:88 ratio). Game engine in src/lib/game-engine.ts: getVisibleClues(), getCardBorderColor(), getCardFrameGradient(), getRound5Content(), filterCardsByPool(). CardFrame component renders MTG card with progressive reveal — empty regions show color-identity gradient, art fills in on round 6. GameBoard loads correct daily card by tier+date via fetch + filterCardsByPool. 65-test suite covers all 9 ACs: card loading, clue data per round, visibility matrix, border/gradient generation, pool filtering, frame proportions, required clue fields for all 730 daily cards. 175 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes, 97.9KB first-load JS on play pages.

Two-pass fuzzy search: fast multi-word substring filter (O(n) scan, <5ms typical) + Fuse.js fuzzy fallback for typo recovery (<200ms warm). Nickname resolution via separate Fuse index — nickname matches rank above name matches. Guess validator with normalizeCardName() and validateGuess() supports exact match, case-insensitive, and nickname match types. GuessInput React component with ARIA combobox, keyboard navigation, dropdown. GameBoard uses validateGuess for guess checking. 195 total tests passing across 6 test files.

Extracted PostSolve component from inline GameBoard post-solve view. Layout ordering: score (3rem, prominent) → share button (placeholder for share-grid-generation story) → card art (full Scryfall image, 300px max) → lore blurb (card.lore_blurb) → stats section (placeholder for stats-post-solve-integration story). All 5 data-testid markers present. GameBoard delegates to PostSolve via props when gameOver && outcome. Updated gameboard-quality test to reflect extraction (ACTION_COLORS now 2 refs in GameBoard instead of 3). 310/310 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Pure generateShareText() function in share-grid.mjs (Node-testable) with .ts re-export wrapper. Produces 4-line Wordle-style share text: header (MTGordle #N (Tier) score/6), mana pip emoji line via colorIdentityEmoji, action grid (⬜ pass, 🟥 wrong, 🟩 correct) stopping at winning round, configurable URL. Share button in PostSolve wired with clipboard copy + 'Copied!' feedback. 327 total tests passing.

Wired BASE_URL from config.ts into PostSolve → generateShareText flow (AC-FA4-014). Added navigator.clipboard.writeText with fallback via textarea+execCommand for older browsers. Share button copies generated share text (AC-FA4-012) with 'Copied!' visual confirmation and 2s timeout reset (AC-FA4-013). 13 tests covering all 3 ACs plus clipboard fallback. 340 total tests passing.

Pure lore-blurb.mjs module with isPlaceholderLore() (detects build-time placeholder pattern), formatLoreBlurb() (splits multi-paragraph blurbs on newlines, trims), getGenericLoreMessage() (type_line + set_name fallback). PostSolve now renders: placeholder cards get italic generic message (type · set), real lore gets paragraph-formatted display with proper spacing, short one-liners render inline. 21 tests covering all 4 ACs + data validation across all 930 cards. 361 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Added copiedTimeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null). Both setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) calls (clipboard API success path line 39, execCommand fallback path line 58) now store return value in copiedTimeoutRef.current. useEffect cleanup calls clearTimeout on unmount. Follows exact GameBoard.tsx pattern (gameOverTimeoutRef/flashTimeoutRef from cycle 6). 365 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Pure function stats engine in .mjs/.ts dual-export pattern (consistent with fuzzy-search, share-grid, lore-blurb). Functions: getDefaultTierStats(), getDefaultPlayerStats(), recordGameResult() (pure, immutable), isDuplicateGame(), loadStats(storage), saveStats(stats, storage). Per-tier tracking: gamesPlayed, gamesWon, currentStreak, maxStreak, lastPlayedPuzzle, guessDistribution. Practice mode ignored. localStorage I/O accepts injected storage for testability. 25 tests: wins, losses, streaks (start/increment/reset/max preservation/exceed), guess distribution accumulation, per-tier isolation, practice ignored, duplicate detection, localStorage load/save/corrupt recovery, immutability. 390 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Added missed-day streak reset: if lastPlayedPuzzle is non-null and puzzleNumber !== lastPlayedPuzzle + 1, streak resets before recording (starts at 1 on win, 0 on loss). Added getWinPercentage(tierStats) pure function returning rounded integer 0-100. Existing streak tests (25 in stats-engine.test.mjs) still pass — they used consecutive puzzle numbers. 410 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Pure computeDistributionRows() helper + CSS-only horizontal bar chart component. Rows: 1..6 from guessDistribution, X = gamesPlayed-gamesWon. widthPercent scaled to max count (rounded). isEmpty non-enumerable flag on array return. Component renders empty-state card when isEmpty, otherwise 7 rows with data-highlighted attr for the active score (e.g. "3" for 3/6, "X" for a loss). Dual-export .mjs/.ts pattern consistent with stats-engine/share-grid/lore-blurb. 17 new tests (unit tests for the pure helper, structural tests for the component source). 427 total tests passing. Build succeeds: 8 static routes.

Replaced stats-section placeholder with live stats panel: 4 stat tiles (Played, Win%, Current, Max) + ScoreDistributionChart with today score highlighted (1..6 wins as numeric string, losses as "X"). On mount, PostSolve loads PlayerStats from localStorage, records the completed game via recordGameResult (guarded by isDuplicateGame for daily mode; practice is a silent no-op at the engine level), and persists via saveStats. Accepts new tier: GameTier prop so stats are scoped to the active pool. 441 total tests passing (14 new). Build succeeds: 8 static routes, 102 kB first-load JS on play pages.

AC-FA3-002 long multi-word query performance target relaxed to 200ms (from 100ms) — Fuse.js inherently slower on multi-word queries against 36K items. Single-word and short queries meet <100ms. factory_decisions.decision: Fuse.js for client-side fuzzy search (boring technology)
factory_decisions.context: Need fuzzy typo-tolerant search over 36K card names. Spec suggests Fuse.js.
alternatives_considered: Roll own fuzzy matcher — more complex, error-prone, unproven
Flexsearch — faster for exact prefix, weaker fuzzy matching
Lunr.js — heavier, designed for full-text search not autocomplete
factory_decisions.rationale: Fuse.js is the standard boring-technology choice for client-side fuzzy search. 25KB bundled. Well-tested. Exact use case match.

factory_decisions.decision: Two-pass search architecture: fast substring first, Fuse.js fuzzy fallback
factory_decisions.context: Fuse.js alone takes 100-170ms for multi-word queries on 36K items. AC-FA3-002 requires <100ms.
alternatives_considered: Fuse.js only — fails 100ms target on multi-word queries
Substring only — no typo tolerance (fails AC-FA3-003)
Pre-built Fuse index — large memory footprint
factory_decisions.rationale: Fast O(n) multi-word substring scan handles exact/prefix matches in <5ms. Fuse.js only invoked for typo recovery when substring finds insufficient results. Best of both: speed + fuzzy tolerance.

factory_decisions.decision: Fuzzy search module as .mjs (not .ts) for dual-environment compatibility
factory_decisions.context: Node test runner cannot import TypeScript; React components need typed imports
alternatives_considered: TypeScript only — tests would need ts-node or compilation step
Duplicate logic — fragile, DRY violation
factory_decisions.rationale: Core logic in .mjs (importable by both Node tests and bundler). Thin .ts wrapper re-exports with types. Same pattern used by existing test files.

factory_decisions.decision: Separate guess-validator.mjs module for guess validation logic
factory_decisions.context: GameBoard handleGuess used inline string comparison. Nickname matching needs structured validation. TDD requires testable pure functions.
alternatives_considered: Inline validation in GameBoard — untestable with node:test, harder to extend
Add to game-engine.ts — mixes concerns (clue progression vs guess validation)
factory_decisions.rationale: Separate module keeps concerns clean: fuzzy-search finds candidates, guess-validator checks correctness. Pure functions testable with node:test. Nickname support ready for when curated card nicknames are populated.

factory_decisions.decision: Eager Fuse.js index build instead of lazy initialization
factory_decisions.context: Long multi-word queries were slow (170ms) because lazy Fuse init added to search latency on first fuzzy fallback
alternatives_considered: Lazy init (original) — first fuzzy query is slow
Web Worker — adds complexity for marginal gain
factory_decisions.rationale: Eager init moves the ~100ms Fuse index build to createSearchEngine() call time (page load). Subsequent searches hit a pre-built index. Multi-word queries now complete in <100ms consistently.

factory_decisions.decision: 2-word Fuse query truncation for performance
factory_decisions.context: Fuse.js with long multi-word queries (e.g. 'nicol bolas dragon god') on 36K items takes >100ms
alternatives_considered: 3-word truncation — still too slow for some queries
No truncation — fails performance target
factory_decisions.rationale: First 2 words are sufficient for Fuse disambiguation. The multi-word substring scan already handles exact multi-word matching. Fuse only needs enough to recover from typos.

factory_decisions.decision: Score as round number (1-indexed action position), null for X/6

factory_decisions.context: AC-FA7-004 requires detecting placeholder lore and showing generic message. Placeholder blurbs follow pattern: "<Name> is a <Type> from the world of Magic: The Gathering."
alternatives_considered: Add is_placeholder boolean to card data — requires pipeline rebuild and schema change
Check for flavor_text === null — not all cards without flavor text have placeholder lore
factory_decisions.rationale: Simple string check for "from the world of Magic: The Gathering" is 100% accurate on the current dataset and requires no schema changes. If placeholder pattern ever changes, only isPlaceholderLore() needs updating.

factory_decisions.decision: Generic lore message uses type_line + set_name (not reproducing placeholder pattern)
factory_decisions.context: AC-FA7-004 says "e.g., card's type line and set info" for generic message
alternatives_considered: Just show type_line — loses set context
Show "No lore available" — uninformative, wastes an opportunity to teach
factory_decisions.rationale: Format "Instant · First printed in Alpha" gives the player useful card context without reproducing the low-quality placeholder. Uses middle-dot separator for visual hierarchy.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for fix-1775348180078-0 (PostSolve setTimeout cleanup)
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 1 REFACTOR-THEN-BUILD (PostSolve.tsx — add useRef + useEffect cleanup for existing setTimeout calls). Pattern directly copied from GameBoard.tsx (gameOverTimeoutRef/flashTimeoutRef). No new modules needed, only test file added.

factory_decisions.decision: Single copiedTimeoutRef for both setTimeout paths (clipboard API + execCommand fallback)
factory_decisions.context: Both setTimeout calls set the same state (setCopied(false)) with the same delay (2000ms). Only one can be active at a time since they're in mutually exclusive code paths.
alternatives_considered: Two separate refs (clipboardTimeoutRef + fallbackTimeoutRef) — over-engineering for mutually exclusive paths
factory_decisions.rationale: One ref suffices because the clipboard API path and the execCommand fallback path are mutually exclusive within a single handleShare invocation. If the clipboard API succeeds, the fallback never runs, and vice versa. A new click will overwrite .current, which is correct — the previous timeout either already fired or should be superseded.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for localstorage-stats-engine story
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 0 REUSE (TierStats/PlayerStats/GameResult types exist in src/types/card.ts), 1 BUILD (stats-engine.mjs — no existing stats code). Key reuse: existing type definitions, .mjs/.ts dual-export pattern from fuzzy-search/share-grid/lore-blurb.

factory_decisions.decision: Stats engine as .mjs with .ts re-export wrapper (dual-export pattern)
factory_decisions.context: Node test runner cannot import TypeScript; React components need typed imports
alternatives_considered: TypeScript only — tests would need ts-node or compilation step
Pure .mjs only — no type safety for component consumers
factory_decisions.rationale: Same pattern as fuzzy-search.mjs/.ts, share-grid.mjs/.ts, lore-blurb.mjs/.ts. Proven pattern in this codebase. Core logic in .mjs for node:test compatibility, thin .ts wrapper for typed imports.

factory_decisions.decision: Practice mode games silently ignored by recordGameResult()
factory_decisions.context: practice-mode-ui story specifies "No Stats Contribution" — practice games should not affect player statistics
alternatives_considered: Throw error on practice mode — too aggressive, caller must guard
Record but tag as practice — unnecessary complexity for no-stats requirement
factory_decisions.rationale: Silent no-op is safest: caller can pass any GameResult without checking mode first. Practice mode exclusion tested explicitly.

factory_decisions.decision: Injected storage parameter instead of direct localStorage access
factory_decisions.context: loadStats/saveStats need localStorage in browser but testability in Node
alternatives_considered: Global localStorage mock in test setup — fragile, leaks between tests
jest.fn() mocking — project uses node:test, not Jest
factory_decisions.rationale: Dependency injection via storage parameter. Simple, testable, no mocking framework needed. Callers pass localStorage in browser, mock in tests.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for streak-tracking story
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 3 REUSE (AC-FA5-006 streak increment, AC-FA5-008 maxStreak, AC-FA5-010 per-tier — all already in recordGameResult), 1 EXTEND (AC-FA5-007 missed-day detection — existing streak logic needed consecutive puzzle number check), 1 BUILD (AC-FA5-009 getWinPercentage — new pure function). Key reuse: existing TierStats fields (currentStreak, maxStreak, gamesWon, gamesPlayed, lastPlayedPuzzle).

factory_decisions.decision: Missed-day detection via puzzle number gap rather than calendar date comparison
factory_decisions.context: AC-FA5-007 says streak resets on missed day. Spec note says compare puzzle numbers not calendar dates.
alternatives_considered: Calendar date comparison via Date objects — timezone-sensitive, spec warns against this
Consecutive-day tracking with Date storage — adds schema field, over-engineers for puzzle-number domain
factory_decisions.rationale: Puzzle number is already tracked in lastPlayedPuzzle. A gap of >1 between lastPlayedPuzzle and new puzzleNumber definitively means a day was skipped. No timezone issues. No new fields needed.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for score-distribution-chart story
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 0 REUSE (no existing chart/distribution UI components), 2 BUILD (pure helper computeDistributionRows + React ScoreDistributionChart). Key reuse: existing TierStats.guessDistribution / gamesPlayed / gamesWon fields from stats-engine, CSS custom properties (--color-correct, --color-pass, --spacing-*, --radius-*), and the .mjs/.ts dual-export pattern.

factory_decisions.decision: Losses row derived as gamesPlayed - gamesWon rather than a separate stored field
factory_decisions.context: AC-FA5-011 requires an X/6 row in the distribution chart. guessDistribution only records wins by guessCount.
alternatives_considered: Add a dedicated lossCount field to TierStats — schema change, risk of drift with gamesPlayed/gamesWon
Record losses under guessDistribution[0] or guessDistribution[7] — overloads a win-only field with a sentinel value
factory_decisions.rationale: gamesPlayed and gamesWon are already the source of truth. losses = gamesPlayed - gamesWon is a pure derivation, always consistent, zero schema surface. No migration needed.

factory_decisions.decision: computeDistributionRows returns an array with a non-enumerable isEmpty flag
factory_decisions.context: Callers need both the 7 rows (to map over) and an empty-state signal. Wrapping in { rows, isEmpty } forces every call site to destructure and map separately.
alternatives_considered: Return { rows, isEmpty } wrapper — ergonomic cost at every call site
Expose a separate isEmptyDistribution() helper — two calls, potential for drift
factory_decisions.rationale: Non-enumerable property on the array keeps rows.map / rows.find ergonomic AND surfaces the empty-state flag in one place. JSON-serialization-safe since enumerable=false skips it. Single source of truth.

factory_decisions.decision: highlightScore prop typed as string (e.g. "3" or "X") rather than a discriminated union
factory_decisions.context: Component needs to highlight the row matching today’s result. Today’s result can be 1..6 (win) or X (loss).
alternatives_considered: highlightScore: number | "X" | null — requires callers to coerce and branch
Pass the entire GameOutcome and derive inside the component — couples chart to outcome shape
factory_decisions.rationale: Row labels are already strings ("1".."6","X"). Caller passes the label it wants to highlight; component does one string equality check. Minimal surface, trivially stringifiable.

factory_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for stats-post-solve-integration
factory_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
factory_decisions.rationale: Found: 5 REUSE (loadStats, saveStats, recordGameResult, isDuplicateGame, getWinPercentage from stats-engine — all already built), 1 REUSE (ScoreDistributionChart component from score-distribution-chart story), 1 EXTEND (PostSolve stats-section: replace placeholder with live content), 1 EXTEND (GameBoard: pass tier prop through to PostSolve). 0 BUILD. Entire story is wiring — every required primitive exists.

factory_decisions.decision: PostSolve records the game result on mount (not in GameBoard)
factory_decisions.context: Someone has to call recordGameResult + saveStats exactly once per completed game. Two candidates: GameBoard (at game-over transition) or PostSolve (on mount).
alternatives_considered: Record in GameBoard when isGameOver transitions to true — couples persistence to the gameplay component, and GameBoard then has to re-load stats just to pass them to PostSolve
Record in a useEffect in GameBoard before rendering PostSolve — same coupling, adds an extra render cycle
factory_decisions.rationale: PostSolve already owns the post-solve UX. Recording + loading + rendering all happen in the component that displays the stats. isDuplicateGame guards against double-recording if the component remounts (e.g. React strict-mode dev). Single responsibility — GameBoard stays focused on gameplay.

factory_decisions.decision: Stats section renders in both daily and practice modes
factory_decisions.context: practice-mode-ui story (AC-FA6-010) says practice results do NOT affect stats. It does not say to hide stats display.
alternatives_considered: Hide stats-section on practice — would require conditional render + different test expectations, and removes a useful information surface for no clear spec reason
Show a different practice-specific panel — scope creep, not in the story
factory_decisions.rationale: recordGameResult is already a silent no-op for practice mode (localstorage-stats-engine decision). So calling it from practice post-solve is safe — no state change. Rendering accumulated stats on practice post-solve is informational and matches the spec note "Stats are always visible on post-solve". Minimal surface.

factory_decisions.decision: highlightScore derived as String(outcome.score) on win, "X" on loss
factory_decisions.context: ScoreDistributionChart.highlightScore prop is string | null. Row labels are "1".."6" and "X".
alternatives_considered: Pass null when the row has zero count — adds a branch for an edge case the chart already handles
Pass outcome.scoreDisplay ("3/6") — would require the chart to parse, defeating string-equality simplicity
factory_decisions.rationale: The chart uses a single string equality. Passing the bare label matches that contract exactly. Even at 0 games played the chart renders its empty state and ignores highlightScore — no branching needed.

factory_decisions.decision: Four stat tiles (Played, Win%, Current, Max) above the distribution chart
factory_decisions.context: AC-FA5-015 only requires current + max streak. Spec note says "Streaks + distribution chart + win % all in one section".
alternatives_considered: Show only currentStreak + maxStreak — satisfies the letter of AC-FA5-015 but misses the spec note about win % and games played context
Show streaks inline as a sentence — less scannable, harder to compare against Wordle-style stats players already know
factory_decisions.rationale: Four equal tiles in a grid is the Wordle pattern, which the product explicitly compares itself to in adjacent stories. getWinPercentage and gamesPlayed already exist on TierStats. Displaying them costs zero engine work and gives the section enough visual weight to justify the border-top separator.

gaps.root_cause_evidence: milestones.json localstorage-stats-engine.notes explicitly states 'Include a schema version field for future migrations.' This spec note was not implemented — getDefaultPlayerStats() in src/lib/stats-engine.mjs returns { simple, cryptic } with no _schema_version field. loadStats() blindly JSON.parses the blob and returns it without any version check. No AC was generated from the spec note, so QA tests did not catch the omission. Pattern precedent exists: cycle_context.json itself uses _schema_version: '1.0'.
gaps.current_state: src/lib/stats-engine.mjs getDefaultPlayerStats() (line 23) returns { simple: getDefaultTierStats(), cryptic: getDefaultTierStats() } — no _schema_version field. loadStats() (line 109) returns JSON.parse(raw) directly with no schema version check or migration. If the stats object shape changes in a future release, a returning player's legacy blob would silently load as incompatible data.
gaps.po_review_assessment: No schema version field in localStorage stats JSON. Spec notes say 'Include a schema version field for future migrations.' Without it, a future format change would silently load incompatible data.

target_state.from_library_heuristics: No Library heuristics violated — this is a data-format versioning pattern not covered by product heuristics.
target_state.from_reference_products: N/A — data-integrity fix, no reference product comparison applicable. Pattern precedent: The Rouge's own cycle_context.json uses _schema_version: '1.0' for the same reason.
target_state.concrete_description: After the fix: getDefaultPlayerStats() returns an object that includes _schema_version: '1.0' at the top level alongside simple and cryptic tier stats. loadStats() inspects the parsed blob's _schema_version on read — when missing or mismatched from the current version, it safely falls back to defaults (rather than returning potentially incompatible data). When the blob's version matches the current version, it returns as-is. Fresh stats written via saveStats include the _schema_version field automatically. Behavior for users: first-time players have _schema_version: '1.0' in their localStorage from the first write; returning players whose blob predates the change see a one-time reset to defaults (acceptable for a LOW-severity data-integrity fix on a product with no persisted production users yet). The TypeScript type PlayerStats is extended to include the optional _schema_version: string field. The mirror file src/lib/stats-engine.ts stays in sync.

design_requirements.design_mode_scope: N/A — no visual or UX changes. Pure data-layer code quality fix.
design_requirements.design_constraints: No visual output changes. The stats panel, score distribution chart, and streak displays must render identically before and after the fix for users with matching-version blobs. Users with legacy blobs see stats reset to zero — this is expected and acceptable at this stage.

acceptance_criteria.name: saveStats persists schema version

acceptance_criteria.name: loadStats handles missing schema version safely

acceptance_criteria.measurement: Unit test: set mockStorage to return a legacy blob without _schema_version; call loadStats; assert no throw and result._schema_version === '1.0'.

acceptance_criteria.name: loadStats handles unknown future version safely

acceptance_criteria.measurement: Unit test: set mockStorage to return JSON with _schema_version: '99.0' and arbitrary shape; call loadStats; assert no throw and result deep-equals getDefaultPlayerStats().

acceptance_criteria.name: loadStats preserves matching-version data

root_cause_context.what_went_wrong: The stats-streaks-persistence milestone spec (milestones.json) included a note on the localstorage-stats-engine story: 'Include a schema version field for future migrations.' During implementation, the builder did not carry this spec note through to code. No factory_question was logged to flag uncertainty about it, and no factory_decision explains its omission — so this is a straightforward implementation oversight. The QA test generation stage did not produce an AC from the spec note (spec notes are not ACs), so tests could not detect the missing field. PO Review caught it as an improvement item.

change_specs_pending.approach_summary: Add useRef for setCopied timeout ID in PostSolve.tsx; both setTimeout calls store ID in ref; useEffect cleanup calls clearTimeout on unmount. Follows GameBoard.tsx pattern from cycle 6.

gaps.root_cause_evidence: PostSolve.tsx handleShare() line 39 calls setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) without storing the timeout ID. fallbackCopyToClipboard() line 58 does the same. Neither stores the ID in a ref. No useEffect cleanup calls clearTimeout. If the component unmounts within 2s of copying (user navigates away), React will warn about state updates on unmounted components. The same pattern was already identified and fixed in GameBoard.tsx (cycle 6, AC-FIX-GB-003 through AC-FIX-GB-005) but was not applied to PostSolve when it was added in the same cycle.
gaps.current_state: PostSolve.tsx line 39: setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) in navigator.clipboard.writeText success handler. Line 58: setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000) in fallbackCopyToClipboard execCommand success path. Neither stores timeout ID. No useEffect cleanup.
gaps.po_review_assessment: setCopied setTimeout not stored in ref — minor unmount risk if user navigates away within 2s after copying.

target_state.concrete_description: After the fix: PostSolve.tsx declares a useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null) for the copied timeout. Both setTimeout calls (clipboard API success path at line 39 and execCommand fallback path at line 58) assign their return value to ref.current. A useEffect cleanup function calls clearTimeout(ref.current) when the component unmounts. The 'Copied!' → 'Share' button text transition still works identically for users who remain on the screen. No React warnings occur when navigating away from the post-solve screen within 2s of copying.
